text: Figure 5.4 Equivalence of least squares and maximum likelihood loss for the normal distribution. a) Consider the fit is good, so these deviations are small (e.g., for the two highlighted points). b) For these parameters, the fit is bad, and the squared deviations are large. c) The least squares criterion follows from the assumption that the model predicts the mean of a normal distribution over the outputs and that we maximize the probability.
